label: Muertes Militares
source: Imperial War Museum / Oxford History of WWI
contestNote: Estimates range from 8.5–11 million depending on source methodology and inclusion of disease/missing

label: Muertes de Civiles
source: Imperial War Museum / Matthew White casualty estimates
contestNote: Figures vary widely; includes famine, disease, genocide, and direct conflict casualties

label: Bajas Totales (incl. heridos)
source: Encyclopedia Britannica / John Keegan, The First World War
contestNote: Wounded counts are disputed; some sources cite 21 million wounded in addition to deaths

label: Longitud del Frente Occidental
source: Hew Strachan, The First World War (Oxford, 2003)

label: Bajas en la Batalla del Somme
source: National Archives UK / Imperial War Museum
contestNote: British, Commonwealth, French, and German casualty figures are separately tallied with varying definitions

label: Bajas en la Batalla de Verdún
source: French Ministry of Defence Archives / Alistair Horne, The Price of Glory
contestNote: French and German tallies differ; German figures often omitted from French national estimates

label: Bajas por Gas Venenoso
source: SIPRI / L.F. Haber, The Poisonous Cloud (1986)
contestNote: Figures include all chemical agents; long-term health effects not fully captured

label: Costo Total de la Guerra (USD 1918)
contestNote: Methodologies for calculating indirect and postwar costs (reparations, debt) vary significantly

label: Prisioneros de Guerra
source: Niall Ferguson, The Pity of War (1998)

label: Barcos Aliados Hundidos por Submarinos
source: Royal Navy Historical Branch / Conway's All the World's Fighting Ships
